title: Human Brain Collection and Cryopreservation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"atvm6","text":"High-quality human brain collection and cryopreservation are essential for enabling reliable single-cell and single-nucleus analyses. Postmortem interval, tissue handling, and rapid stabilization of metabolic and transcriptional states critically determine cell viability, RNA integrity, and preservation of native molecular phenotypes. Best-practice workflows, described here including standardized dissection, immediate cooling, optimized cryoprotectants, and controlled-rate freezing, is described to minimize ischemic stress, degradation, and artifactual activation of vulnerable cell types such as microglia. Consistent documentation and chain-of-custody procedures further ensure reproducibility across donors and studies. Rigorous biospecimen stewardship is therefore foundational for generating high-resolution cellular atlases and for advancing mechanistic insights into human brain aging and disease.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Brain Tissue Harvesting Buffer (see Brain Tissue Buffers) CryoStor‱ CS10 ( https://www.stemcell.com/products/cryostor-cs10.html ) TissueVault Cryogenic Freezing Bag (TV0909) ( https://www.origen.com/products/solid-tissue-cryopreservation/tissuevault-cryogenic-freezing-bag ) Freezing Trays (Origen) Sterile petri dishes Sterile Surgical kit (forceps, scissors, blades) Flacon tubes (15ml and 50ml) Vacuum sealer Igool cool box Ice
Hippocampus at the level of the LGN with adjoining Entorhinal Cortex Substantia Nigra Middle Frontal Gyrus Middle Temporal Gyrus Caudate Cerebellum Cortex (Any)
Place 8x 10ml aliquots (50ml falcon tubes) of Harvesting buffer for tissue collection into Iglool cool box with ice pack Bring the tubes and box over to Autopsy room and leave in the fridge
Thaw 1-2x 35ml aliquots (50ml falcon tubes) of Harvesting buffer for washing
Determine the weight of each brain region
Carefully transfer any one tissue onto, with its Harvesting Buffer, a sterile petri dish Document take a picture
Keep all tissue on ice until ready to process Process one tissue at a time
Chop up tissue into couscous size bits
Prepare TC hood with all required material Sterilize the surgical instruments with 70% ETOH
Transfer the pieces with a wide-mouth transfer pipette to a 15ml falcon tube and keep on ice Continue to process all tissues

Once all tissue has been chopped up and transfered to the falcon tubes Spin tubes at 200 x g, 4 °C, Remove supernatant Add CS10 2x according to volume of tissue A B C D 1 Brain Region Volume of tissue (ml) CS10/ml CS10 (ml) 2 Hippocampus at the level of the LGN with adjoining Entorhinal Cortex 3 2 6 3 Substantia Nigra 3 2 6 4 Middle Frontal Gyrus 4 2 8 5 Middle Temporal Gyrus 5 2 10 6 Caudate 2 2 4 7 Cerebellum 5 2 10 8 Cortex (Any) 5 2 10 9 xxx option one

Label TV0909 Freezing Bag with De-identified ID #, Brain region, Date Transfer the tissue pieces/CS10 suspension to Freezing Bag with a transfer pipette Vacuum seal the tissue pieces/CS10 suspension/Freezing Bag into 2 sections or 4 sections Place o-ring from corning coolcell onto of bag Wrap the whole thing in puppy-pad Store in -80 °C for 20min Unwrap from puppy-pad Place the tissue pieces/CS10 suspension/Freezing Bag to tray Place tray in Liquid nitrogen
creator: Kausalia Vijayaragavan
